clinical_workflow.intended_use: Clinician-controlled documentation scaffold for cold intolerance endocrine documentation.
clinical_workflow.not_for: Not for autonomous diagnosis, treatment selection, dosing, disposition, admission/discharge, procedural, imaging, referral, or emergency management decisions.

source_phase1.reason_for_inclusion: Common Endocrinology / Diabetes / Metabolic documentation scenario suitable as clinician-controlled note scaffolding rather than autonomous clinical advice.
source_phase1.distinct_purpose: Distinct documentation workflow for clinician-selected details around cold intolerance endocrine documentation.
source_phase1.duplicate_check_notes: Cleaned Phase 1 candidate. Exact ID is unique within this revised index and must be checked against existing_workflow_ids.json and alias inventory before import; no close baseline workflow assigned in this cleaned index.

safety_notes: No autonomous diagnosis or treatment recommendation is intended.
Red flags are documentation prompts only.
red_flag_prompts: severe or rapidly worsening symptoms documented if assessed
abnormal vital signs documented if measured
new neurological, cardiorespiratory, or systemic concern documented if assessed
clinician concern requiring escalation documented if present

clinical_blockers: Onset, exposure, systemic symptoms, temperature, thyroid history, medication, examination, laboratory results, diagnosis and plan remain unsupported.
All retained legacy items remain unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction is authorised.
